# Skill: Engineering Workflow Orchestrator

Purpose

Coordinate the complete software development lifecycle by selecting and invoking appropriate engineering skills.

This skill acts as the primary workflow controller for solving engineering tasks and assignments.

It ensures that development proceeds in a structured and verifiable manner rather than ad-hoc code generation.

When to Use

Use this skill whenever a new engineering task, coding assignment, or project specification is provided.

This skill should be executed before any implementation begins.

Responsibilities

1. Interpret the provided task or assignment.
2. Determine the scope and system requirements.
3. Initiate requirement analysis using the appropriate analysis skill.
4. Initiate architecture design and technology selection.
5. Coordinate backend implementation.
6. Coordinate frontend implementation.
7. Ensure authentication and authorization mechanisms are applied where needed.
8. Trigger testing and validation steps.
9. Coordinate deployment of the application.
10. Generate project documentation.

Execution Workflow

Step 1
Analyze the assignment using the requirements analysis skill.

Step 2
Design system architecture and project structure using the architecture planning skill.

Step 3
Implement backend services including database integration.

Step 4
Implement authentication and authorization mechanisms if required.

Step 5
Develop the frontend application and integrate with backend APIs.

Step 6
Perform automated and manual testing to validate functionality.

Step 7
Deploy the system to a publicly accessible environment.

Step 8
Generate technical documentation and setup instructions.

Validation Requirements

After completing each stage:

• verify that generated code compiles
• verify API endpoints respond correctly
• verify database interactions function correctly
• verify frontend communication with backend
• verify authentication flows
• verify deployment accessibility

Development Principles

Ensure the following principles are maintained:

• modular architecture
• clear folder structure
• environment variable usage for sensitive data
• readable and maintainable code
• basic error handling
• minimal but functional user interface

Always prefer widely adopted production-ready frameworks and simple architecture when completing time-constrained assignments.
